  "findings": [
    "Aluminum conductor terminated on a device not rated for CO/ALR",
    "Fire hazard due to galvanic corrosion and thermal expansion mismatch"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Replace the non-compliant device with one rated for CO/ALR",
    "Alternatively, pig-tail the aluminum wire to a copper conductor using an approved connector (e.g., AlumiConn or COPALUM) before terminating on the device"
  ],
  "rationale": "Terminating solid aluminum conductors on devices (receptacles/switches) that are not specifically rated and marked CO/ALR is a severe code violation and fire hazard due to the differing thermal expansion rates and oxidation properties of aluminum and copper.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 406.3(C)",
    "NEC 110.14",
    "NEC Table 310.16"
  ]
}
